calendar.matters:
Petition For The Termination Of Further Proceedings And For Discharge Of The Personal Representative And For An Order (Probate Code 12251)
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Continued To Apr-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James E. Berge for Bruno S. Paulazzo, Executor (via Zoom).

Mr. Berge reported that their client was evicted from the estate property in November/December, and the property is no longer available for inventory. As a result, there are no assets in the estate. The petitioner was the sole beneficiary, there are no creditors, and no one is on special notice. Counsel requested guidance on how to terminate or withdraw the petition.

The court directed Mr. Berge to file a status report explaining the lack of assets and formally requesting that the matter be taken off calendar with prejudice. The matter was continued to April 30, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Continued To Feb-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Berge appeared by zoom for and with Bruno Paulazzo in person.
Christian Paulazzo (Bruno's son) also present.

Bruno stated that he filed for bankruptcy back in November, spoke of his financial circumstances, his employment history, stated that the bank did a foreclosure sale on his home, that he found out that there was fraudulent activity on behalf of the bank and that he is looking for someone to represent him. Bruno also stated that the house was originally in his name, that he put the house in his mother's name to take out a reverse mortgage, spoke of the difficulty he is having in finding an attorney because they are all requesting a $5,000.00 retainer, stated that the last mortgage was at about $900,000.00 to $1,000,000.00, that the lender has not been in contact with him and requested a six month continuance. Mr. Berge stated that he thinks there was a reverse mortgage deed of trust on the property, that he believes the foreclosure sale was done in violation of the stay, that it's probably safe to believe that the sale could be set aside, that the buyer of the property has never filed to evict and that his client is between a rock and a hard place. Mr. Berge also stated that he believes there may be $100,000.00 in equity and that he would like to see his client at the least benefit from the equity on the home. Christian stated that he has been to the Bar Association at least seven times. Status on close of estate is continued to February 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to show cause is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-29-2024 Continued To Aug-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Berge appeared by zoom for Bruno Paulazzo (petitioner).

Mr. Berge stated that he was prepared to file a petition, that it was ready to go and that his client refused to sign it because he believes there are procedural issues regarding the foreclosure sale of the house. Mr. Berge also stated that his client is seeking legal advice with respect to the foreclosure sale to see if he has a case, that there are no assets in the estate and that his client is still living in the house even though it has been sold. The court informed Mr. Berge that he will need to file a petition to terminate the estate for lack of assets. Status on close of estate is continued to August 7, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition to terminate the estate is not filed by then, Bruno Paulazzo will be required to appear. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executor Bruno Paulazzo to appear on August 7, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he is refusing to sign a petition.

Judge: Roger T. Picquet. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Continued To May-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Berge appeared by zoom for Bruno Paulazzo.

Mr. Berge stated that the house was lost to foreclosure in November. Status on close of estate is continued to May 29, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Berge is to file a petition to terminate estate for lack of assets by May 15, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated January 20, 2022 is admitted to probate. Bruno S. Paulazzo is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for April 3, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, no appearance will be required on that date. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.
